summary.intent: Run the implemented AI metrics dashboard application and validate all functionality using Playwright E2E tests alongside existing unit tests.
summary.outcome: All 148 unit tests passed and Playwright E2E tests confirmed the dashboard renders correctly with all 4 sections, 20 metrics, 42 chart canvases, proper computed values, and a working cache-missing error state.

repo: The AI metrics dashboard is an Astro project running on port 4322 (not default 3000/4321).
Dashboard has 4 sections: Attribution, Token Economics, Quality Signals, and Behavioral/SDLC.
Charts use a mix of Chart.js and lightweight-charts — 42 canvas elements total, 4 of which are lightweight-charts containers.
The app reads from src/data/checkpoints-cache.json; missing cache shows an error card instructing users to run `npm run fetch-checkpoints`.
Seed data with 5 checkpoints produces: Pure-AI Commit Rate = 40% (2/5 checkpoints), Zero-Human-Touch Lines = 840 (450 from beta + 390 from epsilon).
CoinGecko API warnings in dev are expected — no live API available in development mode.
The Astro dev toolbar generates a benign console error for `dev-toolbar/entrypoint.js` that is unrelated to application code.

code.finding: Seed data file created during this session to enable E2E testing without running the fetch script.
Contains computed metric logic; verified Pure-AI Commit Rate and Zero-Human-Touch Lines calculations are correct against seed data.
Script that populates checkpoints-cache.json; must be run before the app can display data in production.

workflow: Starting the dev server in the background before running Playwright allows E2E and manual inspection to proceed in parallel.
Testing cache-missing state requires temporarily removing the cache file, then restoring it — a manual toggle approach.
Playwright browser evaluate calls are effective for asserting DOM structure (canvas counts, section headings, absence of error messages) without writing a full test file.

friction: ToolSearch had to be called multiple times to locate the correct Playwright MCP tool names, indicating tool discovery is non-trivial.
The `mcp__plugin_playwright_playwright__browser_navigate` tool name inconsistency (double `playwright`) suggests unstable MCP plugin naming.
open_items: No formal Playwright test file was written — E2E coverage exists only as ad-hoc browser evaluate calls from this session.
The deferred-work.md artifact was modified, suggesting there are known deferred tasks tracked separately that were not addressed in this session.
sprint-status.yaml was updated, implying sprint tracking is ongoing and this session represents one completed task among others.
